items.headline: Dallas Stars Minnesota Wild game 3 recap April 22 2026
items.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. – Wyatt Johnston scored a power-play goal at 12:10 into the second overtime to give the Dallas Stars a 4-3 win against the Minnesota Wild in Game 3 of the Western Conference First Round at Grand Casino Arena on Wednesday.

items.headline: 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs first round schedule television results
items.description: The 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs are underway. Here is the complete schedule for the first round of the playoffs, including TV information and results:

items.headline: Philadelphia Flyers Game 3 home win worth wait
items.description:
PHILADELPHIA -- The Philadelphia Flyers’ fans waited more than 10 years for this.
Trevor Zegras, Rasmus Ristolainen, Nick Seeler and Noah Cates waited their entire careers.
The wait was worth it.
Zegras, Ristolainen, Seeler and Cates each scored the first Stanley Cup Playoff goal of his career to propel the

items.headline: Anaheim Ducks Edmonton Oilers Game 2 recap April 22, 2026
items.description: EDMONTON -- Cutter Gauthier scored his first two Stanley Cup Playoff goals, including the go-ahead goal late in the third period, to lift the Anaheim Ducks to a 6-4 victory against the Edmonton Oilers in Game 2 of the Western Conference First Round at Rogers Place on Wednesday.

items.headline: NHL announces start times for Stanley Cup Playoffs 1st Round games on April 28
items.description:
NEW YORK – The National Hockey League today announced the following updates to the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs First Round schedule:
- The start time for Game 5 of the First Round series between the Boston Bruins and Buffalo Sabres has been set for 7:30 p.m. ET on Tuesday, April

items.headline: Pittsburgh Penguins Philadelphia Flyers Game 3 recap April 22, 2026
items.description: PHILADELPHIA -- Trevor Zegras and Noah Cates each had a goal and an assist for the Philadelphia Flyers, who took a 3-0 series lead against the Pittsburgh Penguins with a 5-2 win in Game 3 of the Eastern Conference First Round at Xfinity Mobile Arena on Wednesday.

items.headline: Vikings Justin Jefferson hypes up Minnesota Wild fans Game 3
items.description:
Justin Jefferson had the crowd go wild at Grand Casino Arena on Wednesday.
The Minnesota Vikings wide receiver led fans in a pregame chant before Game 3 of the Western Conference First Round between the Minnesota Wild and Dallas Stars.

items.description:

items.headline: Minnesota Wild Mats Zuccarello Yakov Trenin Game 3 injury updates
items.description:
ST. PAUL, Minn. -- Mats Zuccarello and Yakov Trenin are each out for the Minnesota Wild in Game 3 of the Western Conference First Round against the Dallas Stars at Grand Casino Arena on Wednesday (9:30 p.m. ET; HBO MAX, FDSNWI, FDSNNO, Victory+, truTV, TNT, SN360, TVAS).
Each forward was

items.headline: Comedian Shane Gillis Philadelphia Flyers Eastern 1st Round
items.description:
Comedian Shane Gillis got things going at Xfinity Mobile Arena on Wednesday, before the Philadelphia Flyers and Pittsburgh Penguins dropped the puck in Game 3 of the Eastern Conference First Round.
Gillis – a Flyers superfan – was joined by team mascot Gritty to “ignite the orange” before the game.

items.headline: Buffalo Sabres look to pick up pace in Game 3
items.description:
BUFFALO -- The Buffalo Sabres know what their identity is.
They play with speed and pace from the first line to the fourth line and the top defense pair to the third pair. They can strike quickly, especially with an active and mobile back end, not allowing the opposition time

items.headline: Senators Sanderson ready for increased workload in Game 3 against Hurricanes
items.description:
KANATA, Ontario -- Everywhere you looked in Game 2 of the Eastern Conference First Round, there was defenseman Jake Sanderson.
He played a game-high 43:06 for the Ottawa Senators in a 3-2 double-overtime loss to the Carolina Hurricanes at Lenovo Center.
It's an astounding number, but it is on par

items.headline: Canadiens ready for more playoff hockey in Montreal
items.description:
MONTREAL -- Hockey, they say in these parts, is a religion.
That claim isn’t just Montreal Canadiens fans talking the talk. To their credit, they walk the walk.
Right into their own personal house of worship.

items.headline: Colorado Avalanche Martin Necas continuing unreal season in playoffs
items.description:
DENVER -- Martin Necas held the puck for one extra moment or two. The Colorado Avalanche forward then flicked a pass between the legs of Los Angeles Kings goaltender Anton Forsberg to a wide-open Gabriel Landeskog, who scored the game-tying goal with 3:35 remaining in the third period.
